title: [CI:DOCS] Clean up man pages.
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: I had a lot more of these that I lost when I screwed up with git, so I figured I would attempt to get at least these merged and will go back to the rest of the man pages when I am bored.

LGTM at first pass, but -- I know it's probably way late for this -- what do we think about replacing period with colon in all of these? I find it *ultra* confusing to read a long list of examples-and-descriptions because it takes me several seconds to figure out if the description applies to the lines above or below. A colon is an unobtrusive and unambiguous indicator.
Well I have to do a much larger pass through all man pages examples to fix them up, so I would rather do this on a followup PR. (Next airplane flight.)

Through out, I think where you say "the {container, volume, pod, etc} {name, id, key}" that the value for {name, id, key} should be backticked.   Plus it looks like you might have missed some of @edsantiago 's earlier comments.
I think we need to take multiple passes through this. Still lots to fix, Thanks @edsantiago and @TomSweeneyRedHat for reviewing.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/lgtm
/hold

I found several typos in the new revision, and some unclear wording, but I am keeping them to myself because I do not want to go through another iteration of this. I will submit a followup -- smaller -- PR. And please, next time, can we break these down into smaller chunks?

/unhold
Good I will get back and look for missing descriptions. @edsantiago please open a PR with your fixes, and then if you got a script to change the `.` to `:`.
